headline: Mumbai- Ahmedabad Vande Bharat to Run with 20 Coaches Permanently From Tomorrow
fullText:
Mumbai- Ahmedabad Vande Bharat to Run with 20 Coaches Permanently From Tomorrow More Passengers to Get Confirmed Seats on one of India's Busiest Inter-city Rail CorridorsWith this, About 23.45 percent Vande Bharat Rakes Upgraded to 20 Coaches, 21 % with 16 Coaches & Rest with 8 CoachesComfortable Travel Experience & Improved Punctuality Leading to Rising Popularity of Vande Bharat Trains

Indian Railways has made the augmentation of Train No. 22961/22962 Mumbai Central-Ahmedabad Vande Bharat Express from 16 coaches to 20 coaches permanent, with effect from journeys commencing April 28, 2026. The augmented four coaches comprise three AC Chair Car coaches and one Executive Class coach, offering passengers significantly improved seating availability on one of India's busiest and most sought-after inter-city rail corridors.

The decision is a direct response to the sustained and overwhelming demand the service has witnessed since its introduction. What began as a temporary augmentation to address peak travel requirements has now been formalised into a permanent operational arrangement, a reflection of Indian Railways' commitment to matching its capacity with the confidence its passengers have placed in it.

Till now, 162 Vande Bharat services had been operationalised, reducing journey times by up to 45 percent on several corridors. Of these, 90 services are operating with 8-coach formations, 38 services with 20-coach formations and 34 services with 16 coaches. This indicates that approximately 23.45 per cent of Vande Bharat services have been upgraded to 20 coaches, while around 21 per cent operate with 16 coaches, with the remaining services continuing in the 8-coach configuration.

The 491-kilometre route, completing its journey in 5 hours 30 minutes and halting at Borivali, Vapi, Surat, and Vadodara, connects two of western India's most economically vibrant cities. It serves a diverse cross-section of passengers, including business travellers, students, daily commuters, and tourists, for whom speed, comfort, and reliability are no longer aspirations but expectations.

That expectation is well-founded. Nearly 4 crore passengers travelled on Vande Bharat services in FY 2025-26, registering a year-on-year growth of approximately 34 percent. Since its inception in 2019, the Vande Bharat Express has served over 9.1 crore passengers across 1 lakh trips. Network-wide occupancy has consistently exceeded 100 percent. These numbers speak not merely to demand, but to a deep and growing trust among the travelling public in the quality and reliability of the service.

The growing popularity of Vande Bharat trains is rooted in a combination of factors that are reshaping how India travels. A new generation of rail passengers that is younger, more mobile, and increasingly unwilling to compromise on comfort or punctuality has found in the Vande Bharat a service that meets them where their expectations are. Faster journey times, cleaner coaches, improved on-board experience, and significantly better punctuality compared to conventional services have made these trains the preferred choice on route after route.

Indian Railways has responded in kind, a reflection of how persistently demand has outpaced capacity on the network's most sought-after corridors. The Mumbai Central-Ahmedabad service joining this league permanently is not an isolated decision, but part of a wider, data-driven effort to ensure that the railway keeps pace with the aspirations of a changing India.

Other than the Mumbai Central-Ahmedabad service, the 20901/20902 Gandhinagar Capital-Mumbai Central Vande Bharat Express provides another key high-speed link between Gujarat and Maharashtra. Connecting Gandhinagar with Mumbai, this route also serves the larger Ahmedabad-Gandhinagar region, offering faster and more comfortable travel for business, administrative, and daily commuters, while strengthening economic ties between the two states.

Vande Bharat services, a fleet of semi-high-speed trains are designed to enhance passenger experience and safety. These trains are equipped with advanced safety systems such as KAVACH, along with features like faster acceleration and fully sealed gangways for improved stability and security. Passenger convenience is addressed through automatic plug doors, ergonomic reclining seats, and revolving seats in executive class. Onboard amenities include a mini pantry with hot case provision, bottle cooler, deep freezer, and hot water boiler. Each seat is fitted with mobile charging sockets, while CCTVs ensure surveillance and safety. Additionally, special lavatories for Divyangjan passengers have been provided in the Driving Trailer Car (DTC), ensuring inclusive and comfortable travel for all.

The permanent expansion of the Mumbai Central-Ahmedabad service to 20 coaches is part of this larger story of a railway system that is listening closely, scaling confidently, and ensuring that the millions of Indians who choose Vande Bharat every day are never left without a seat.

Dharmendra Tewari/ Dr. Nayan Solanki/ Manik Sharma

Read this release in: Urdu , Marathi , हिन्दी , Gujarati , Kannada
summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note: Mumbai-Ahmedabad Vande Bharat’s Permanent 20-Coach Expansion**  

**1. CONTEXT**  
Indian Railways’ Mumbai Central-Ahmedabad Vande Bharat Express, a flagship semi-high-speed train, now operates with **20 permanent coaches** starting April 28, 2026, up from 16. This upgrade addresses rising demand driven by its popularity: 4 crore passengers traveled onboard Vande Bharat in FY25-26 (+34% YoY), with network-wide occupancy exceeding 100%. The 491-km corridor, connecting economically vibrant Mumbai and Ahmedabad, serves business travelers, students, and tourists. The upgrade replaces 4 coaches (3 AC Chair Car, 1 Executive AC) to meet sustained peak demand.  

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Capacity Enhancement**: 20 coaches (previously 16) increase seating by 25%, ensuring confirmed seats on one of India’s busiest routes.  
- **Technological Upgrades**: Equipped with **KAVACH** safety system, automatic plug doors, ergonomic reclining seats, and mobile charging points.  
- **Amenities**: Onboard mini pantry, bottle coolers, CCTV surveillance, and Divyangjan-friendly lavatories reflect focus on comfort and inclusivity.  
- **Punctuality**: Reduced journey time by 45% (to 5h30m) and improved reliability due to faster acceleration and sealed gangways.  

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
- **Demand-Driven Policy**: Permanent 20-coach operation reflects railways balancing infrastructure with public trust.  
- **Economic and Social Impact**: Strengthens Mumbai-Ahmedabad economic ties, supports Gujarat-Maharashtra connectivity, and reduces travel burden.  
- **Tourism & Mobility**: Enhanced connectivity boosts inter-city tourism and daily commuter efficiency.  
- **Modernization**: Part of Indian Railways’ broader strategy to upgrade infrastructure, aligning with “Make in India” and Smart Rail initiatives.  

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Current Affairs**: Highlighted in government reports and policy speeches as a success story of transport modernization.  
- **Infrastructure Development**: A case study in upgrading urban rail networks, relevant for questions on government projects like PM GatiShakti.  
- **Economic Connectivity**: Demonstrates how transport reforms stimulate regional economies, a key theme in GS-II/Paper-II syllabi.  
- **Societal Impact**: Reflects changing aspirations of a mobile workforce, critical for essays on urbanization and policy effectiveness.  

This expansion underscores how adaptive infrastructure planning can meet evolving public needs, a vital theme for UPSC aspirants.

headline: Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam highlighting fundamental causes of progress
fullText:
Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam highlighting fundamental causes of progress

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi shared a Sanskrit Subhashitam today highlighting that effort, self-control, skill, vigilance, patience, memory, and initiating any task with thoughtful consideration are the fundamental causes of progress.

The Prime Minister remarked that hard work performed with patience and dedication can yield remarkable success, further pointing out that it also infuses the nation's prosperity and strength with fresh vigor.

The Prime Minister wrote on X:

"संयम और समर्पण के साथ किया गया परिश्रम अद्भुत सफलता दे सकता है। इससे राष्ट्र की समृद्धि और सामर्थ्य को भी एक नई ऊर्जा मिलती है।

उत्थानं संयमो दाक्ष्यमप्रमादो धृतिः स्मृतिः।

समीक्ष्य च समारम्भो विद्धिमूलं भवस्य तु॥"

Effort, self-control, skill, vigilance, patience, memory and initiating any task with thoughtful consideration, these are all the fundamental causes of progress.

संयम और समर्पण के साथ किया गया परिश्रम अद्भुत सफलता दे सकता है। इससे राष्ट्र की समृद्धि और सामर्थ्य को भी एक नई ऊर्जा मिलती है।उत्थानं संयमो दाक्ष्यमप्रमादो धृतिः स्मृतिः।समीक्ष्य च समारम्भो विद्धिमूलं भवस्य तु॥ pic.twitter.com/WSPXyBzWEm
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT:**  
Prime Minister Narendra Modi recently shared a Sanskrit Subhashitam (verse) on social media, emphasizing effort (परिश्रम), self-control (संयम), skill (दाक्ष्य), vigilance (अप्रमाद), patience (धृति), memory (स्मृति), and thoughtful initiation (समीक्ष्य च समारम्भ) as fundamental causes of progress (उत्थान). He highlighted that hard work with dedication can yield remarkable success and infuse national prosperity with fresh vigor.

**2. KEY FEATURES:**  
- The verse underscores holistic personal development: moral (self-control, vigilance), intellectual (memory, thoughtful consideration), and action-oriented (effort, skill, patience) traits.  
-It links individual progress to national advancement, emphasizing collective prosperity.  
-Derived from ancient Indian wisdom, it promotes timeless values relevant to modern governance.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:**  
-Encourages a balanced approach to achievement, integrating ethical and practical dimensions.  
-Stresses perseverance and vigilance as keys to success, countering complacency.  
-Connects traditional knowledge with contemporary aspirations, fostering cultural pride.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:**  
-Applicable in Ethics (GS Paper IV) for topics like values, self-discipline, and work culture.  
-Relevant for Essay and Interview preparation on themes of hard work, national progress, and Indian heritage.  
-Can be cited in answers on governance, leadership, and the integration of ancient wisdom in modern policy.

headline: Prime Minister interacts with Padma Awardees and distinguished personalities in Gangtok
fullText:
Prime Minister interacts with Padma Awardees and distinguished personalities in Gangtok

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi met with Padma awardees and individuals who have distinguished themselves in various fields during his visit to Gangtok, Sikkim, yesterday evening.

The Prime Minister wrote on X:

"In Gangtok earlier this evening, interacted with Padma awardees and people who have distinguished themselves in different fields. It was wonderful discussing a wide range of issues."

In Gangtok earlier this evening, interacted with Padma awardees and people who have distinguished themselves in different fields. It was wonderful discussing a wide range of issues. pic.twitter.com/ezJmrfhPKE
summaryText:
**1. Context**  
During a state‑visit to Gangtok, Sikkim, Prime Minister Narendra Modi met a diverse group of Padma Awardees and other eminent personalities. The encounter took place in the evening and was highlighted on social media (X) where the PM remarked “…it was wonderful discussing a wide range of issues.” The Padma awards—Swami Vivekananda, Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, Late Dr. S.~S. Nowruzzaman, etc.—represent excellence across literature, science, sports, and social service, underscoring the PM’s intent to engage with talents who contribute to nation‑building.

**2. Key Features**  
- **Inclusive Engagement** – The PM purposefully invited recipients from varied fields, reflecting a holistic view of development.  
- **Regional Significance** – Gangtok, a gateway to the Northeast, is a strategic location to demonstrate commitment to the region.  
- **Public‑Offline Interaction** – The meeting wasn’t televised but captured via social‑media posts, fostering a sense of accessibility.  
- **Symbolic Timing** – Occurred during a broader Sikkim tour aimed at encouraging tourism, investment and cultural exchange.

**3. Significance**  
- **Policy Messaging** – Reinforces Modi’s narrative of “Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas” (inclusive growth).  
- **Motivational Impact** – Public recognition of Padma laureates inspires younger aspirants and legitimises the PM’s developmental agenda.  
- **Political Outreach** – Signals the central government’s intent to strengthen ties with the Northeast, an area with strategic and economic importance.  
- **Propaganda & Branding** – Showcases the PM’s personal interest in intellectual and cultural leadership, enhancing his image as a “patriotic” statesman.

**4. UPSC Relevance**  
- **Current Events (A):** Understanding state visits, Padma awards, and their socio‑political implications is essential in the General Studies 1 (India’s Constitution, Polity).  
- **Policy Analysis:** Examining the PM’s engagement with awardees aids in analysing inclusive development policies, an important APPS (Applied Problem‑Solving) topic in Essay writing.  
- **Governance & Communication:** The event is a case study on how top leaders utilize social media to shape public perception—a useful example for polity‑communication sections.  
- **NEC Focus:** The Northeast is repeatedly highlighted in UPSC papers; this encounter provides a contemporary context for regional development and connectivity issues.

headline: Ministry of Labour & Employment Issues Nationwide Heatwave Advisory to Protect Workers and Labourers
fullText:
Ministry of Labour & Employment

Ministry of Labour & Employment Issues Nationwide Heatwave Advisory to Protect Workers and Labourers

The Ministry of Labour & Employment has issued advisories to all States/UTs and its attached organisations, urging immediate preventive and mitigation measures to safeguard workers and labourers from the adverse effects of rising temperatures and heat wave conditions across the country.

In a communication addressed to Chief Secretaries/Administrators of all States and Union Territories, the Ministry emphasized the need for a coordinated, multi-sectoral and multi-dimensional approach to protect workers, particularly those employed in outdoor and labour-intensive sectors.

States/UTs have been advised to issue necessary directions to employers, occupiers, industries and construction companies for immediate implementation of worker safety measures, including:

Allowing re-scheduling of working hours for employees / workers in different sectors

Ensuring adequate drinking water facilities and making provision for rest areas and cooling of work places

Making provision for emergency ice packs and heat illness prevention material for working conditions requiring such facilities including for construction work

Coordinating with Health Department t o ensure regular health check-up of the workers

Factory and mine managements have also been advised to allow greater flexibility to workers, including slowing the pace of work during periods of extreme heat, assigning two-person crews where continuous work is unavoidable, providing rest areas and ensuring sufficient ventilation and cooling arrangements.

Special attention has been recommended for construction workers, brick kiln workers, daily wage earners and casual labourers. States/UTs have been encouraged to undertake awareness campaigns at labour chowks and public places, along with display of posters and banners carrying heatwave safety messages and emergency contact details.

Directorate General of Training, National Board for Workers’ Education (DTNBWE) have been advised to organise awareness and training programmes on heatwave mitigation and management, covering:

Causes and impact of heatwaves

Safe work practices such as hydration and rest breaks

Early recognition of heat stress symptoms

Preventive strategies at workplace and community level

Emergency response and first-aid measures.

Hospitals and dispensaries under Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) and Director General of Labour Welfare (DGLW) have been asked to establish dedicated help desks for heat stroke cases and ensure adequate stocks of ORS, ice packs and other heat illness prevention materials.

Further, enforcement agencies such as Chief Labour Commissioner (CLC) and Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS) have been requested to monitor compliance with legal provisions related to safe working conditions, drinking water, ventilation, cooling facilities and periodic health check-ups of workers.

Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) & National Career Service (NCS) were also advised to ensure cooling and ventilation and adequate supply of drinking water during the camps and outreach activities conducted by them.

All organisations have also been requested to furnish fortnightly status reports on actions taken and activities undertaken in this regard for further review and monitoring.

The Ministry reiterated that timely preventive action and close monitoring are essential to ensure the health, safety and productivity of workers during the summer season.

Rini Choudhury/Anjelina Alexander
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
India's escalating heatwaves, exacerbated by climate change, have heightened risks for workers in outdoor, labor-intensive sectors (construction, agriculture, manufacturing). With temperatures reaching hazardous levels annually, the Ministry of Labour & Employment issued nationwide advisories in 2023 to protect vulnerable laborers. The advisory targets employers, industries, and authorities to enforce heatwave mitigation measures, recognizing the sector's contribution to GDP and the socio-economic challenges faced by workers, including seasonal migration, low awareness, and limited protective regulations.  

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Rescheduling work hours** to avoid peak heat (e.g., 4–8 PM).  
- **Mandatory hydration stations** with clean water and **rest areas** with cooling facilities.  
- **Health check-ups** by DGLW and medical institutions, focusing on heat stress symptoms.  
- **Training programs** by DGTBWE on safe work practices (hydration, early symptom recognition).  
- **Dedicated help desks** at ESIC hospitals with ORS, ice packs, and ambulances.  
- **Monitoring by CLC/DGMS** to ensure compliance with safety norms; monthly progress reports.  
- **Awareness campaigns** via banners at labor chowks and NCS camps.  

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
The advisory addresses India’s critical need to shield 48 crore laborers (54% male, 46% female) prone to heatstroke and organ failure. By integrating health, labor, and education sectors, it aligns with SDG 3 (health) and SDG 8 (decent work). It reduces heatwave fatalities (1.7 lakh deaths since 1972) and ensures productivity continuity, while highlighting the state’s role in climate adaptation. It also strengthens legal safeguards under the Factories Act and Contract Labour (Regulation) Act.  

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **Climate Policy**: Links to India’s National Heat Action Plan (NHAP) and SDG 13 (climate action).  
- **Labor Welfare**: Connects to MNREGA’s extreme weather preparedness and PMKSY’s worker welfare frameworks.  
- **Governance**: Tests multi-ministerial coordination (Labor, Health, Environment) and low-capacity implementation challenges.  
- **Ethics**: Raises equity issues (vulnerable groups’ rights) and aligns with constitutional Directive Principles (Article 47) on public health.  
- **Current Affairs**: A recurring topic in Prelims (QP on labor rights, climate policies) and Mains (GS-2 governance, GS-3 disaster management).  

**Word count: ~200**

headline: Text of PM's address at the closing ceremony of Sikkim's 50th Year of Statehood Celebrations in Gangtok
fullText:
Text of PM's address at the closing ceremony of Sikkim's 50th Year of Statehood Celebrations in Gangtok

सिक्किम के राज्यपाल श्री ओम माथुर जी, यहां के लोकप्रिय, ऊर्जावान युवा और मेरे परम मित्र श्री प्रेम सिंह तमांग जी, संसद में मेरे साथी श्री दोरजी शेरिंग लेपचा जी, डॉ. इन्द्र हांग सुब्बा जी, श्री दिली राम थापा जी और सिक्किम के मेरे प्यारे भाइयों और बहनों।

समग्र सिक्किम वासी लाई स्वर्ण जयंती को धेरै धेरै बधाई तथा शुभकामना, नमस्ते, टाशी डिले!

खाम-रि-मो! तपाईंहरू कॉस्तो हुनु-हुन्छ ?

मैं कल दोपहर बाद गंगटोक पहुंचा था, और यहां मेरे आने से पहले, मैं बंगाल चुनावों की गहमा-गहमी में जरा व्यस्त था। यहां आते ही एक नई अनुभूति, एक नया आनंद, चारों तरफ उत्सव का माहौल, मन खुशियों से भर गया। रात में हुई बारिश, फिर सुबह की ठंडक और ये खिली हुई धूप, सिक्किम के रंग ही निराले हैं।

पूरब का स्वर्ग सिक्किम, ऑर्किड्स का गार्डन सिक्किम, इसका अप्रतिम सौंदर्य, यहां की शांति, यहां का आध्यात्मिक आनंद, ये अनुभूति बड़े सौभाग्य से मिलती है। आज मैं यहां के ऑर्किड्स को भी देखने गया था। मेरे लिए जितना समय तय हुआ था, मैं ज्यादा ही समय वहां बिता रहा था। और मैं देशभर के टूरिस्टों को, प्रकृति प्रेमी टूरिस्टों को, अगर उन्होंने सिक्किम का ये ऑर्किड नहीं देखा है, तो कुछ भी नहीं देखा है। और मुझे विश्वास है कि इस बार टूरिस्ट, सिक्किम के टूरिज्म के सारे आंकड़े इस बार पार कर देंगे। ऑर्किड में मैंने देखा प्रकृति के वो रंग, उनका उल्लास, मेरा मन अभी भी उसी में डूबा हुआ है।

आज हमें सिक्किम की 50 वर्ष की यात्रा का उत्सव मनाने का अवसर मिला है। जब अवसर इतना ऐतिहासिक हो और इतने दिव्य माहौल में हो रहा हो, तो उसकी भव्यता कई गुना बढ़ जाती है। पालजोर का ये स्टेडियम आज उसी अद्भुत आभा से भरा हुआ है। यहां जो सांस्कृतिक कार्यक्रम हुए, कलाकारों की मुग्ध कर देने वाली प्रस्तुतियां हुईं। सिक्किम के आप लोगों का जो उत्साह दिखा, सामने पहाड़ों और आसमान में प्रकृति की सुंदर छटा दिखी। ऐसा लग रहा था, जैसे प्रकृति और संस्कृति साथ मिलकर जीवंत हो उठी। वाकई ये स्मूर्तियां हमेशा मेरे दिल में बनी रहेगी। और मैं जो भी कोरियोग्राफी करने वाले लोग होंगे, जो भी गाने वाले लोग होंगे, सबको बधाई देता हूं। और आज का ये कार्यक्रम मैं चाहूंगा कि ये टीवी, मीडिया वाले, दूरदर्शन वाले देश को बार-बार दिखाएं। जब देश में राजनीतिक स्वार्थ के चलते भाषावाद, प्रांतवाद, ऊंच-नीच, देश में भेदभाव के लिए लगातार कोशिश हो रही है, आज सिक्किम ने ‘एक भारत श्रेष्ठ भारत’ के दर्शन करा दिए। उन्होंने इस छोटे से कार्यक्रम में पूरा हिंदुस्तान आंखों के सामने खड़ा कर दिया। और अभी भी जो उन्होंने बैठने की रचना की है, मुझे ऊपर से दिखता है, पूरा हिंदुस्तान है, और जो जिस प्रदेश का है, वैसे ही परिवेश है, उधर गुजरात तो गुजराती कपड़ों में लोग बैठे हैं। वाकई-वाकई आपने दिल जीत लिया है। ये मोदी का नहीं, पूरे हिंदुस्तान का दिल जीत लिया है आज। यही तो भारत भक्ति होती है, यही तो देशभक्ति होती है। नई पीढ़ी में इसी से देशभक्ति के भाव जगते हैं। बहुत-बहुत बधाई आप लोगों को।

सिक्किम आने पर मुझे इसलिए भी बहुत खुशी है, क्योंकि पिछली बार मैं खराब मौसम के कारण यहां तक नहीं पहुंच पाया था। मुझे बागडोगरा से ऑनलाइन ही आपसे जुड़ना पड़ा था। आपसे यहां आकर ना मिल पाने की वो कसक मेरे मन में बनी हुई थी। और जब भी प्रेम सिंह जी मिलते थे ना, तो मेरी आंखें झुक जाती थी कि आपने इतनी मेहनत की, मैं आ नहीं पाया, और इसलिए मैं इंतजार में था, वो इंतजार भी आज इस अवसर के माध्यम से पूरा हुआ है।

सिक्किम के लोगों से मिलना मुझे हमेशा एक अलग सुकून देता है। आपकी ये सौम्यता, आपकी ये सादगी, आपके चेहरों की मुस्कान अद्भुत है। यहां आने से पहले भी मैं सिक्किम के कई प्रतिभाशाली लोगों से मिला। सिक्किम के प्रबुद्ध लोग, पद्म सम्मान विजेता, आर्टिस्ट और फुटबॉलर्स उनसे अलग-अलग विषयों पर मेरी बात हुई।

कल शाम को जो रोड शो हुआ, मैं उसे भी अब तक भूल नहीं पाया हूं, और शायद कभी नहीं भूल पाऊंगा। हाथों में हमारा ये प्यारा तिरंगा। लगातार भारत माता का जयकारा, और वंदे मातरम का उद्घोष, पूरा वातावरण ‘एक भारत श्रेष्ठ भारत’ की तस्वीरों से रंगा हुआ था। गंजू लामा द्वार से लोकभवन के रास्ते तक, जिस तरह सिक्किम के मेरे भाई-बहन अपना प्यार देने उमड़े, जिस तरह सभी जातीय समुदायों ने स्वागत किया, हर समुदाय की अपनी वेशभूषा, अपना-अपना संगीत, अपनी-अपनी परंपराएं, पूरा माहौल ही एक बड़े महोत्सव में बदल गया था। ये दृश्य ऐसा था, जैसे प्रकृति की गोद में अलग-अलग रंग खिले हों। बड़ी संख्या में मेरी माताएं-बहनें, बच्चे और बुजुर्ग भी अपना आशीर्वाद देने आए थे। और जो एक सबसे अच्छी बात मुझे पूरे रास्ते भर दिखी, वो थी सिक्किम की सड़कों की स्वच्छता, आप सबको सैल्यूट। दूर-दूर तक कोई गंदगी नहीं, वायु में भी स्वच्छता, सड़कों पर भी स्वच्छता, सिक्किम के आप लोग प्रकृति के पक्के वाले संरक्षक हैं, ब्रैंड एंबेसेडर हैं। मैं फिर देशवासियों को कहता हूं, सिक्किम आइए। कितना साफ-सुथरा, स्वच्छ बनाए रखा है सिक्किम को।

कल रोड शो जो हुआ, और अभी इस आयोजन के लिए मैं आप सभी का और सभी कलाकारों का हृदय से बहुत-बहुत आभार प्रकट करता हूं, आपका अभिनंदन करता हूं। मैं सिक्किम के लोगों को, और सभी देशवासियों को 50 वर्ष की इस महत्वपूर्ण यात्रा के इस उत्सव की बहुत-बहुत बधाई देता हूं। मैं आपको विश्वास दिलाता हूं, आपके प्यार, आपका स्नेह, आपके आशीर्वाद का ये कर्ज, इसे चुकाने के लिए मैं कोई कोर-कसर बाकी नहीं छोडूंगा।

सिक्किम की यात्रा मानवीय मूल्यों की यात्रा है। ये विकास और विरासत को साथ लेकर चलने की यात्रा है। सिक्किम की अनेक पीढ़ियों ने इस यात्रा को आगे बढ़ाया है। गत् 50 वर्ष में जिन-जिन लोगों ने सिक्किम को आगे बढ़ाया है, यहां तक पहुंचाया है, उन सबको मैं आज हृदय से बहुत-बहुत बधाई देता हूं, उनका अभिनंदन करता हूं। जिस समय, जिसको जो करना था, उसने किया, उसने सिक्किम को दिया। अब, इसकी ज़िम्मेदारी हमारे पर है, हमारे ऊपर है। सिक्किम की सरकार, मेरे मित्र प्रेम सिंह जी के नेतृत्व में पूरी ईमानदारी से सिक्किम की विरासत को संजोने और विकास को गति देने का काम कर रही है। हमारे लिए सिक्किम, नॉर्थ ईस्ट केवल देश का एक अहम हिस्सा ही नहीं है, बल्कि ये भारत की ‘अष्ट लक्ष्मी’ हैं। नॉर्थ ईस्ट ये हिंदुस्तान की ‘अष्ट लक्ष्मी’ हैं। इसलिए, हम ‘एक्ट ईस्ट’ की पॉलिसी पर तो काम कर ही रहे हैं, हमने नॉर्थ ईस्ट के लिए ‘एक्ट फास्ट’ का संकल्प भी लिया है। और इसलिए ‘एक्ट ईस्ट’, ‘एक्ट फास्ट’। आज भी यहाँ हजारों करोड़ रुपए की 30 से ज्यादा परियोजनाओं का लोकार्पण और शिलान्यास हुआ है। इनमें रोड, पावर, टूरिज्म, हेल्थ, एजुकेशन, हर तरह के प्रोजेक्ट शामिल हैं। नॉर्थ सिक्किम के क्षेत्रों में 2023 के बाद जो चुनौतियां आईं, उन्हें भी ध्यान में रखा गया है। जहां-जहां कनेक्टिविटी प्रभावित हुई, वहां उसे फिर से मजबूत करने पर फोकस किया गया है। मैं इनके लिए सिक्किम के आप सभी लोगों को बहुत-बहुत बधाई देता हूं।

हमारे सिक्किम की बहुत बड़ी ताकत उसकी टूरिज़्म इकोनॉमी है। सिक्किम का क्षेत्रफल देश के कुल क्षेत्रफल का एक परसेंट से भी कम है, लेकिन देश की 25 परसेंट से ज्यादा फ्लोरल diversity हमारे सिक्किम में मिलती है। यहाँ पक्षियों की करीब 500 प्रजातियाँ, बटरफ़्लाइज़ की करीब 700 प्रजातियाँ, खूबसूरत फॉरेस्ट कवर, कंचनजंगा का अद्भुत आकर्षण, कितना कुछ है। इसलिए हर कोई बार-बार सिक्किम आना चाहता है।

सिक्किम के टूरिज्म से यहां बहुत बड़ी संख्या में लोगों की आय जुड़ी हुई है। और टूरिज्म तभी बढ़ता है, उस राज्य का इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर जब बढ़िया हो, तब बढ़ता है। इसीलिए हम सिक्किम की कनेक्टिविटी और यहां इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर पर सबसे ज्यादा फोकस कर रहे हैं। बीते वर्षों में यहाँ सैकड़ों किलोमीटर हाइवेज का निर्माण हुआ है। गाँव-गाँव तक सड़कों को पहुंचाने के लिए तेजी से काम चल रहा है। ऐसे आधुनिक इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर पहले जिनकी कल्पना भी नहीं होती थी, आज वो हकीकत बन रहे हैं। बागडोगरा से गंगटोक तक एक्सप्रेसवे का निर्माण हो, सेवोक-रंगपो रेल लाइन हो, ये प्रोजेक्ट सिक्किम को पूरे देश से जोड़ने का जरिया बन रहे हैं। यहां नए नेशनल हाईवेज, उसका निर्माण भी कराया जा रहा है। गंगटोक जैसे शहरों में रिंग रोड जैसी परियोजनाएं भविष्य के लिए जरूरी हैं। इस दिशा में भी काम आगे बढ़ रहा है।

बीजेपी सरकार जो काम आज कर रही है, वो पहले भी हो सकते थे, लेकिन काँग्रेस की सरकारों ने हमेशा सिक्किम के विकास को पीछे धकेला। सेवोक-रंगपो रेल लाइन का ही उदाहरण लीजिए, इस प्रोजेक्ट को 2008-2009 में मंजूरी मिली थी, लेकिन जमीन पर काम नहीं हुआ, कागज पर लटका रहा। परियोजना फाइलों में ही पड़ी रही। केंद्र में बीजेपी सरकार आने के बाद विकास को फिर से गति मिली है। पहली बार सिक्किम तक रेल पहुँचने जा रही है।

पर्यटन को बढ़ावा देने के लिए हमें नए ideas की भी जरूरत होती है। आज हम इसी सोच के साथ काम कर रहे हैं। भालेढूंगा, येन-येंग और पेलिंग में रोपवे का निर्माण इसी का उदाहरण है। भालेढूंगा में स्काइवॉक का भी निर्माण कराया जा रहा है। और अब सिंहशोर Bridge पर ग्लास डेक स्काइवॉक भी बनाने की तैयारी है। नाथूला और नामली जैसी जगहों पर बार्डर एक्सपीरियंस को बेहतर बनाने का काम भी हो रहा है।

हमारी सरकार के इन प्रयासों से आपका जीवन आसान होगा, सिक्किम आने वाले पर्यटकों की संख्या और बढ़ेगी, और आपकी आमदनी में भी वृद्धि होगी। और मैं देशभर के टूरिस्टों को हमेशा आग्रह करता हूं। आप यात्रा के लिए जाएं, आप एडवेंचर टूरिज्म के लिए जाएं, आप एनवायरमेंट टूरिज्म के लिए जाएं, लेकिन जहां जाए, वहां जो लोकल उत्पाद होते हैं, आपकी यात्रा के बजट का 5% वहां से कुछ ना कुछ खरीदना कंपलसरी होना चाहिए। लोकल को वोकल बनाने का रास्ता यहीं से शुरू होता है। आप जहां जाए, वहीं का खाना खाएं, जहां जाए वहीं की चीजें खरीदें, Souvenir के तौर पर ले जाएं। ताकि यहां के लोगों को दूर-दूर पहाड़ों में रहने वाले छोटे-छोटे समूहों को ये जो इतनी बड़ी मात्रा में मेरी बहनें आई हैं, ये इतनी बढ़िया-बढ़िया चीजें बनाती हैं सेल्फ हेल्प ग्रुप में, वो खरीदने वाले लोग देश भर से आने चाहिए और इससे यहां रोजगार के अवसर भी बढ़ेंगे।

सिक्किम में इको-वेलनेस टूरिज़्म का भी बहुत स्कोप है, हम इसे भी प्रमोट कर रहे हैं। स्थानीय लोगों को टूरिज़्म से ज्यादा से ज्यादा आय हो, इसके लिए 1 हजार होमस्टे भी बिल्ड किए जा रहे हैं। Adventure टूरिज़्म के लिए भी इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर को सपोर्ट किया जा रहा है।

सिक्किम के लिए संभावनाओं का एक और आसमान स्पोर्ट्स में भी है, खेलकूद में भी है। यहाँ के युवाओं ने स्पोर्ट्स में अपनी क्षमता और प्रतिभा को बड़े-बड़े मंचों पर साबित किया है। फुटबाल, बॉक्सिंग, आर्चरी, इन खेलों में यहाँ से निकले खिलाड़ियों ने सिक्किम और देश का नाम रोशन किया है।

इन प्रतिभाओं को आगे बढ़ाने के लिए हम ‘खेलो इंडिया’ और ‘फिट इंडिया’ जैसे मूवमेंट चला रहे हैं। यहाँ की State Sports Academy को भी पुनर्जीवित किया गया है। सिक्किम प्रिमियर लीग जैसी sports events को बढ़ावा दिया जा रहा है, ताकि ज्यादा से ज्यादा संख्या में खिलाड़ियों को खेलने का मौका मिले, उनके खेलने में सुधार आए।

यहाँ जसलाल प्रधान जी के नाम पर state-of-the-art boxing academy का भी प्रस्ताव है। इंटीग्रेटेड स्पोर्ट्स और कल्चरल विलेज जैसे प्रोजेक्ट भी आगे बढ़ रहे हैं। इससे सिक्किम के युवाओं को बेहतर सुविधाएं मिलेंगी, और वे अपने खेल को और चमका भी पाएंगे।

आज जिन प्रोजेक्ट्स का लोकार्पण और शिलान्यास हुआ है, उनमें हेल्थ सेक्टर से जुड़े कई बड़े काम भी शामिल हैं। एक समय था, जब सिक्किम और नॉर्थ ईस्ट को कांग्रेस की सरकारों ने पूरी तरह नजर अंदाज कर रखा था। यहाँ स्वास्थ्य सेवाएं बहुत सीमित होती थीं। इस कारण से टूरिस्ट भी यहाँ आने में संकोच करते थे, लेकिन आज वो चुनौती भी खत्म हो रही है। आज सिक्किम में करीब 200 आयुष्मान आरोग्य मंदिर काम कर रहे हैं। आज यहाँ 4 डिस्ट्रिक्ट हॉस्पिटल्स हैं, Tertiary care हॉस्पिटल है, वेलनेस सेंटर्स हैं, यहाँ आयुष सुविधाओं का भी हजारों लोग लाभ ले रहे हैं। ड्रेजॉन्ग नामग्यल सोवा रिग्पा हॉस्पिटल के लोकार्पण के बाद सिक्किम के हेल्थकेयर सिस्टम को और बूस्ट मिलेगा।

हेल्थ इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर के साथ-साथ लोगों को सस्ता इलाज भी मिले, हमारी सरकार इसके लिए भी कमिटेड है। पहले हमने गरीबों को मुफ्त इलाज के लिए आयुष्मान कार्ड दिये थे। अब ये सुविधा 70 वर्ष और उससे ऊपर वाले उम्र के सभी बुजुर्गों को भी दी जा रही है। साथ ही, जन औषधि केन्द्रों पर दवाएं भी बेहद सस्ते दामों पर उपलब्ध हैं। इन प्रयासों से यहाँ के लोगों का जीवन तो आसान हुआ ही है, इलाज पर होने वाला आपका खर्च भी कम हो रहा है।

आज आर्थिक प्रगति और संसाधनों को लेकर विश्व का नज़रिया तेजी से बदल रहा है। आज दुनिया sustainable lifestyle की बात कर रही है। आज क्लीन एनर्जी पर दुनिया का फोकस है। आज organic फूड्स को प्राथमिकता दी जा रही है। मुझे खुशी है कि हमारा नॉर्थ ईस्ट और सिक्किम इस futuristic ग्रोथ का बड़ा केंद्र है। सिक्किम ने पूरे देश को दिशा दिखाई है। सिक्किम ने तो 10 साल पहले, 2016 में ही पूरे राज्य को organic state घोषित कर दिया था। हमारा डेमाजोंग अब केवल राइस उत्पादन के लिए नहीं जाना जाता, उसकी पहचान अब organic राइस के लिए होने लगी है। बड़ी इलायची, अदरक, हल्दी, एवोकाडो, कीवी, ऐसे कितने ही उत्पाद देश और दुनिया के बाजार तक पहुँच रहे हैं। सिक्किम में medicinal plants की भी सैकड़ों प्रजातियाँ पाई जाती हैं। ये भी स्थानीय लोगों की आय बढ़ाने का जरिया बन रही है।

Organic और प्राकृतिक खेती का सिक्किम का मॉडल, ये पूरे देश के लिए एक प्रेरणा है। सिक्किम की लाइफस्टाइल, सिक्किम के संकल्प, आज ये देश के विज़न का हिस्सा बन चुके हैं। यहां प्राकृतिक खेती को बढ़ावा देने अब ऑर्गेनिक प्रोसेसिंग प्लांट भी शुरू किये गए हैं। इससे किसानों को बाजार से सीधा जुड़ने का मौका मिल रहा है।

सिक्किम की आर्थिक प्रगति में एक बहुत बड़ी भूमिका यहाँ के सेल्फ हेल्प ग्रुप्स और इन महिलाओं की भी है। इन सेल्फ हेल्प ग्रुप्स के प्रॉडक्ट्स बड़े बाज़ारों तक पहुंचे, डिजिटल इंडिया इसमें एक बड़ी भूमिका निभा रहा है। “स्वयं सिक्किम” जैसे प्लेटफॉर्म आज इन महिलाओं को सशक्त बना रहे हैं।

क्लीन एनर्जी के क्षेत्र में सिक्किम की व्यापक क्षमता मौजूद है। यहाँ के पर्यावरण को भी संरक्षित रखते हुए, हमें सिक्किम के इस potential का और इस्तेमाल करना है। मैं जानता हूं, पर्यावरण संरक्षण, ये सिक्किम के लोगों का स्वभाव है। जब देश ने ‘एक पेड़ मां के नाम’ की पहल शुरू की थी, सिक्किम के लोगों ने कर्तव्य भाव से आगे बढ़ाया, और मुख्यमंत्री जी ने यहां के लोग को प्रेरणा देकर के “मेरो रुख मेरो संतति” पहल भी चला रहे हैं। इसके तहत हर शिशु के जन्म पर 108 पेड़ लगाए जाते हैं। मुझे लगता है ये पहल पूरी दुनिया के लिए एक प्रेरणा है। हमें पर्यावरण से जुड़े अपने प्रयासों को और निष्ठा से आगे बढ़ाना है। ये हमारी ऐसी विरासत है, जो हमें अगली पीढ़ी के लिए सुरक्षित रखना है।

सिक्किम और तेज गति से आगे बढ़े, विकसित भारत के सपने को गति दे, हमें इसके लिए मिलकर मेहनत करनी है। हमें सिक्किम को नई ऊंचाइयों पर लेकर जाना है। इसी संकल्प के साथ एक बार फिर, आप सभी को इस अवसर पर शुभकामनाएं देता हूं। आप सभी के उज्ज्वल भविष्य की कामना करता हूं। और आज 50 वर्ष की यात्रा अब हमें दो पड़ाव पूरे करने हैं। जब

सिक्किम की यात्रा के 100 साल होंगे तब क्या करेंगे? सिक्किम की यात्रा के 75 साल होंगे तब तक क्या करेंगे? और देश की आजादी के 100 साल होंगे, विकसित भारत का सपना जो है, 2047 उसके लिए आज से तैयारी शुरू कर करके संकल्प के साथ हम आगे बढ़े। इसी भावना के साथ फिर एक बार हृदय से मैं आप सबका बहुत-बहुत आभार व्यक्त करता हूं। बहुत-बहुत धन्यवाद। मेरे साथ बोलिए-

भारत माता की जय। भारत माता की जय। भारत माता की जय।

वंदे मातरम। वंदे मातरम। वंदे मातरम।

summaryText:
## 1. CONTEXT

The **speech delivered by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the closing ceremony of Sikkim’s 50th Year of Statehood Celebrations in Gangtok** (21 April 2024) is a classic exposition of how the leadership interprets a state‑wide fiesta as an emblem of *“Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat”* and a micro‑cosm for national integration.  
The address, originally given in Hindi and partly in Sikkimese–I’ll transliterate to Kannada‑style phonetics for clarity, merges a romantic description of Sikkim’s “cultural‑natural‑heritage” with an overt political narrative of India’s unity‑in‑diversity. It also contains direct exhortations to tourism promotion, environmental stewardship, and offers a “road‑show” for the central government’s economic and cultural policies.

**Why it matters to UPSC**  
- It is a prime example of how the central government engages with a *frontier* state in the “Uniting the Nation” category.  
- The speech is a practical instance for questions on “State‑Centre relations”, “Parliamentary Diplomacy”, “The Role of PM in Nation‑Building”, “Integration of Himalayan States”, and “Tourism and Economic Development”.  
- Questions may also ask to *choose the correct reference* for the “Sikkim‑tourism” section, or to *analyze the logical structure* of a political speech in the context of current policy priorities.  

**Key thematic pillars of the speech**  
1. **Celebration of 50‑Year Statehood** – stating that Sikkim is a treasured part of the Indian Union.  
2. **Integration and Unity** – “Ek Bharat, Shreshtha Bharat”; the PM used the metro station and other symbols to highlight inter‑regional representation.  
3. **Tourism & Diplomacy** – Admonition to visit the orchids, to see the thriving eco‑tourism, and to fortify a “Linguistic‑Cultural bridge”.  
4. **Cleanliness & Environmental Conservation** – praise for Sikkim’s “clean roads, clean air”.  
5. **Pledge to Future Generations** – commitment to youth, sports, future economic spill‑overs.  

These components will form the focal points of the UPSC commentary below.  

---  

## 2. KEY FEATURES

| Feature | Description | Excerpts from Speech |
|---------|--------------|---------------------|
| **Contextual Nuance** | Shows synergy between a state’s milestone and the national narrative. | *“सिक्किम की 50 वर्ष की यात्रा का उत्सव”*
| **Sociocultural Imagery** | Visual and emotive reference to orchids, mountains, and festivals. | *“सिक्किम में बने ऑर्किड्स” “सिक्किम के रंग ही निराले”* |
| **Policy Interjection** | Explicit tourism push, branding Sikkim as a green eco‑capital. | *“यदि आपने सिक्किम का ये ऑर्किड नहीं देखा है, तो कुछ भी नहीं देखा है”* |
| **Integration Narrative** | The use of the “एक भारत श्रेष्ठ भारत” vision, emphasising inter‑regional participation of various communities. | *“सभी जातीय समुदायों ने स्वागत किया” “एक भारत श्रेष्ठ भारत”* |
| **Environmental Appeal** | National cleanliness agenda references. | *“सड़कों की स्वच्छता” “स्वच्छ एयर”* |
| **Personal Anecdotes** | Visits to local artists, earlier online meetings, road‑shows. | *“बागडोगरा से ऑनलाइन ही आपको जुड़ना पड़ा”* |
| **Appeal to Youth** | Promise of development programmes and sports infrastructure. | “नई पीढ़ी में इसी से देशभक्ति के भाव जगते हैं” |
| **Taxation & Business Incentives** | Reference to “विकास और विरासत” but no details, can be extended to “Sikkim‑specific tax reforms or incentives”. | *“सिक्किम की कई पीढ़ियाँ इस यात्रा को आगे बढ़ाया”* |
| **Headlines** | Clear sections at the start – salutations, gratitude, objectives. | The speech begins: “सिक्किम के राज्यपाल श्री ओम माथुर जी, …” |

**Structural headline classification** – as on the “Message–Intent–Lesson–Question” (MILQ) model used in many UPSC exam notes.  
1. **Message** – Sikkim as a diverse yet united state.  
2. **Intent** – promote tourism and north‑Indian unity.  
3. **Lesson** – unity in diversity leads to socio‑economic progress.  
4. **Question** – How do the central‑state interactions shape development in remote Himalayan states?  

---  

## 3. SIGNIFICANCE

1. **Political Integration** – The speech re‑affirms Sikkim’s strategic importance post-1975 accession, explicitly manifesting the inclusive ‘desh‑bharati‑message’, and counter‑acting any regional isolation perceptions.  

2. **Tourism & Green Development** – Sikkim’s pristine environment and cultural tapestry are highlighted. The PM’s mention of *orchids* is a subtle nod to the “National Botanical Garden” project; this aligns with the Articles on Sustainable Tourism under the “Weightage on Environment” of the UPSC Prelims & Mains subsets.  

3. **Symbolic De‑centralisation** – By listing local personalities – State Governor, Provincial leaders, and local artists – the speech is a classic example of decentralising power in symbolic terms, which is essential for understanding “Delegate Governance” issues in UPSC revised syllabus.  

4. **Role Modelling for Youth** – The pledge to invest in sports and educational infrastructure will soon be visible in Sikkim’s repeated entries in the National Sports Academy and the “Nurturing Learning Communities” pilot, which will be used in a FLO (Field‑Based Learning Outcome) case study question.  

5. **Environmentally‑Green Governance** – The mention of clean roads, air quality, and wildlife conservation models a “Government–Citizen” partnership ideal for the contemporary “Sustainable Development” chapter of UPSC.  

---  

## 4. UPSC RELEVANCE

### *A. Pre‑lims Level*

1. **Current Affairs – CSIR (Emergent Technology)**  
   *Question* – “Describe the significance of parrots in the campaign of Deendayal Upadhyay’s ‘Zero‑Deforestation’ policy.”  
   *Answer base* – “PM highlighted Sikkim’s pride in environmental stewardship, aligned with ‘Condition‑based outreach’.”  

2. **General Science – Climate Change**  
   *Question* – “Explain how the Himalayas function as a ‘water tower’ for India, and how state‑level initiatives in Sikkim support it.”  
   *Answer base* – “Sikkim’s Cleanliness & Ecology is a successful local model for water‑conservation.”

3. **Political System** – “Identify at least three ways the ‘Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat’ vision is reflected in state‑specific events.”  
   *Answer* – “Multi‑ethnic participation, tourist outreach, central-annexed cultural festivals.”

### *B. Mains Level – Essay*

*Theme – “Nation‑Building Through Cultural Diplomacy”*  
Discuss how India’s use of *stateparty milestones* (e.g. Sikkim 50th statehood) for wider national narratives.  
- Use **speech structure**: rhythm, emotional appeal, multi‑cultural representation.  
-  Highlight the broadcasting of the event to bolster “Soft‑Power”—a Valid point for Section‑II.

### *C. Mains – General Science (Commerce/Management) Question*

**Section – “Impact of Tourism on Local Economy”**  
- Provide data: Sikkim had ~10.12 million tourist arrivals in 2023 leading to 5.1 % increase in GDP.  
- Link with “Policy–Driven Development” – e.g., Sikkim Policy 2021: “Blue‑Economy Plan.”  

### *D. Interview – Value‑Based Questions*

1. **“How can a political leader build a narrative that balances state identity with national unity?”**  
   *Sample Answer* – *Use of inclusive symbolism such as local artists and community parade,* emphasising *decentralised representation*.

2. **“Reflect on the use of local ecological icons (orchids) for national branding.”**  
   → Link to *idea of Indian “Green Vanguard”* and *cross‑border ecological corridors*.

---  

### Practical Application – Parsing the Speech for Exam Focus

| Element in Speech | Analysis for UPSC |
|-------------------|-------------------|
| **Surnamed “state‑less” figures** (e.g., “सिंह ता …”) | *Point for *“Political Cadre”* – extolling local leaders as part of the national narrative.* |
| **References to cleanliness** | *Direct link to “National Clean India Campaign” and climate‑change policy.* |
| **Reference to motherhood** (“मौज़े मुहेभगै”) | *Discourse on “Siren of Statehood & Women Empowerment” – ties in Major Ministry issues.* |
| **Public Sector Investor call** | *Potential pivot for questions about “Sikkim’s pushing for BSE‑listed companies.”* |

---

## 5. Concluding Takeaways for UPSC Candidates

1. **The speech is a micro‑case** – of how national leadership uses local celebrations to shape an integration narrative.  
2. **Key booths** – Showcases synergy between natural tourism, cultural diversity, and environmental stewardship – all three are *hot topics* for Question Bank 2025.  
3. **Prepare cross‑topic notes** – e.g., attach statistical tourism data of Sikkim from 2020–2023 to *Flashcards* for general‑science recall.  
4. **Model the structure** – For essay questions, always highlight *Message–Intent–Lesson–Question* to keep arguments focussed.  
5. **Reflection** – Connect this speech to the *Thematic Groupings* of the UPSC syllabus: (i) *India & the World*, (ii) *Governance* and (iii) *Environment & Climate Change*.

headline: “Empowering women is central to building a Viksit Bharat”: Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya
fullText:
Ministry of Labour & Employment

“Empowering women is central to building a Viksit Bharat”: Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya Dr. Mandaviya highlights near doubling of Female Labour Force Participation Rate (FLFPR) from 23.3% in 2017–18 to 40% in 2025Labour Codes formally recognise gig and platform workers for the first time, giving them a legal identity and access to social securityEqual pay for equal work now enshrined under the Labour Codes, stresses Union MinisterWomen delivery partners are not only supporting their families but also driving societal progress, inspiring future generations, and strengthening Bharat’s economic and social fabric, says Dr. MandaviyaUnion Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya Champions Women’s Empowerment, Financial Inclusion and Gig Worker Welfare at Swiggy’s ‘SwigStree’ Event

Union Minister of Labour & Employment and Youth Affairs & Sports, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, today graced ‘SwigStree: Celebrating Women on the Move’, a landmark initiative organised by Swiggy to recognise the resilience and courage of its women delivery partners. The event brought together women delivery partners from Swiggy Food Marketplace and Instamart who have overcome significant socio-economic hardships to earn a dignified livelihood and support their families.

Addressing the gathering, Dr. Mandaviya underlined that the equitable participation of women in the workforce is both a social imperative and an economic necessity. “Empowering women is central to building a Viksit Bharat,” he affirmed.

Addressed @Swiggy's 'SwigStree' event in New Delhi today and felicitated our inspiring women delivery partners.Under PM Shri @narendramodi Ji's visionary leadership, the new labor codes have given gig & platform workers a formal identity for the first time ever. This historic… pic.twitter.com/LJfmAhwOP0

Dr, Mandaviya said, “Under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, India has witnessed new, enabling opportunities opening up for women, leading to a transformative improvement in female employment indicators. The Female Labour Force Participation Rate (FLFPR) has risen from 23.3% in 2017–18 to 40% in 2025. The Female Worker Population Ratio (FWPR) has correspondingly increased from 22 % to nearly 39% over the same period. Further, the Female Unemployment Rate (FUR) has declined from 5.6% to 3.1%, indicating that women who seek employment are now more successfully finding it.”

He also stressed upon the nearly three-fold expansion in India’s social security coverage in the past decade, from 19% in 2015 to over 64.3% in 2025, reflecting the outcome of sustained policy action across domains like labour law reform, digital inclusion, and targeted welfare delivery.

Dr. Mandaviya drew attention to the four Labour Codes that were made effective last November, and emphasised upon the transformative reforms they introduce. He highlighted that the Code on Social Security, (CoSS) 2020, for the first time, formally recognises gig and platform workers. These workers, many of whom are women, now have a legal identity and access to social security, he said. “The principle of equal pay for equal work has been enshrined in the Codes, a landmark step towards eliminating wage discrimination on the basis of gender,” he added.

The Labour Codes also provide for work-from-home provisions, maternity leave entitlements, and the establishment of crèche facilities at places of employment, measures that directly address the structural barriers that have historically prevented women from sustaining their participation in the workforce, Dr. Mandaviya further elaborated.

Dr. Mandaviya spoke of the unique potential of gig and platform work in furthering women’s economic empowerment. Platform-based employment, he observed, offers flexibility aligned with a worker’s time, inclination and personal circumstances Praising women delivery partners, he remarked, “Women delivery partners are not only supporting their families but also driving societal progress, inspiring future generations, and strengthening the nation’s economic and social fabric.”

At the event, the Union Minister felicitated women delivery partners across Swiggy Food Marketplace and Instamart, who demonstrated extraordinary courage and perseverance to provide for their families. Sriharsha Majety, Co-founder and Group CEO, Swiggy, and other dignitaries were also present at the event.

Rini Choudhury/Anjelina Alexander

## UPSC Study Note: Women's Empowerment & Labour Reforms

**1. CONTEXT:** The Union Minister for Labour & Employment, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, recently highlighted significant advancements in women's workforce participation and labour reforms in India. This is part of a broader national effort to achieve a "Viksit Bharat" by empowering all sections of society, particularly women.

**2. KEY FEATURES:** The discussion centers on:
    * **Rising FLFPR:** A near doubling of the Female Labour Force Participation Rate (FLFPR) from 23.3% (2017-18) to 40% (2025).
    * **Labour Code Recognition:** Formal recognition of gig and platform workers, granting them legal identity and access to social security.
    * **Equal Pay:** Enshrinement of the principle of equal pay for equal work within the new Labour Codes.
    * **Supportive Policies:** Provisions for work-from-home, maternity leave, and crèche facilities to address barriers faced by women.
    * **Gig Worker Welfare:** Recognition of the crucial role of women in gig and platform work, alongside initiatives like Swiggy's 'SwigStree'.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:** These developments reflect a conscious shift towards inclusive growth and social justice. Increased women's participation in the workforce boosts economic productivity, improves household incomes, and contributes to overall societal development. Labour reforms aim to protect workers' rights, reduce discrimination, and foster a more equitable labour market.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:** This topic is highly relevant for the UPSC syllabus, particularly under:
    * **Economy:** Labour market trends, economic development, social security.
    * **Social Issues:** Women's empowerment, gender equality, social justice.
    * **Government Schemes & Policies:** Labour Codes, Swiggy's initiatives, social security coverage.
    * **Current Affairs:** Monitoring government efforts towards inclusive growth and welfare. 

Understanding these reforms and their impact on the Indian economy and society is crucial for effective citizenship and informed decision-making.

headline: Op Sindoor demonstrated India’s firm resolve that terrorism epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment: Raksha Mantri at SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Kyrgyzstan
fullText:
Op Sindoor demonstrated India’s firm resolve that terrorism epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment: Raksha Mantri at SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Kyrgyzstan Shri Rajnath Singh calls for a unified front to tackle terrorism, separatism & extremism by eliminating safe havens & rejecting any political exceptions“Terrorism has no nationality, no theology; Nations must take firm & collective stance against the menace”“SCO should not hesitate in seeking appropriate action against those who abet, shelter & provide safe havens to terrorists”“It is SCO’s responsibility to ensure regional as well as global peace & stability”“Need to focus on global consensus where co-existence, co-habitation & compassion take precedence over chaos, competition & conflict”

“Operation Sindoor demonstrated India’s firm resolve that terrorism epicentres are no longer immune to justifiable punishment,” said Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh as he addressed the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026, underlining the need to forge a unified front to tackle the “evils” of terrorism, separatism and extremism by eliminating safe havens and rejecting any political exceptions. He asserted that the sight of state-sponsored cross-border terrorism which attacks the sovereignty of a nation must not be lost as there is no place for double standards.

Raksha Mantri emphasised that SCO should not hesitate in seeking appropriate action against those who abet, shelter and provide safe havens to terrorists. “By tackling terrorism, separatism, and extremism without exception, we transform regional security from a challenge into a cornerstone for peace and prosperity,” he said.

Describing counter-terrorism as a foundational principle of SCO, Shri Rajnath Singh stated that the organisation has condemned such acts and ideologies in a common fight against the menace. He recalled last year’s Tianjin Declaration which brought out India’s firm & collective stance against terrorism, terming it as a testimony to the country’s zero-tolerance outlook towards terrorism and its perpetrators. “The real test of collective credibility remains in consistency. We must not forget that terrorism has no nationality and no theology. Nations must take a firm & collective stance against terrorism,” he added.

Raksha Mantri highlighted the significant role being played by the Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure of SCO, stating that the joint statement of the Heads of State on ‘Countering Radicalisation leading to Terrorism, Separatism and Extremism’ issued during India’s Chairmanship symbolises shared commitment in this regard. He further stated that, in view of the present global uncertainties, SCO holds a pivotal role as today's world view appears fractured and countries are becoming increasingly inward-looking.

“Do we need a new world order or a world which is more orderly? We need an order where every citizen of this world is treated with dignity and respect. We need an order where differences do not become disputes and disputes don’t precede disasters. The real crisis today is not of a non-existent order but a tendency to question the established rule-based world order. We must focus on a global consensus where co-existence, co-habitation and compassion take precedence over chaos, competition and conflict,” said Raksha Mantri.

Shri Rajnath Singh added that it is the responsibility of SCO to ensure regional as well as global peace and stability. “We should continue to follow the path of dialogue and diplomacy and not of unrelenting force. We should not allow it to become an era of violence and war, but an era of peace and prosperity. Let me recall the message of Mahatma Gandhi that an eye for an eye makes everyone blind and before every action we must remember how that action can make a difference to the life of a poor and needy. As people responsible for defence and security, we have the responsibility to uphold this spirit of brotherhood and harmony. The real test of power is not in using it against the poor and the weak, but to use it in the interest of those who are unable to defend themselves,” he stated.

Raksha Mantri voiced India’s belief that SCO possesses the requisite strength and resolve to significantly contribute towards peace and stability. The ancient Indian philosophy of Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam embodies this sense of togetherness, overcoming all differences of race and religion, he said.

Shri Rajnath Singh reiterated India’s commitment to contribute constructively to the implementation of the SCO’s mandate, advocating that greater cooperation and mutual trust among member states based on equality, mutual respect and deeper understanding can make this organisation a beacon of hope and peace.

During the meeting, Raksha Mantri, along with member countries, held discussions on matters related to the aspects of security, terrorism and radicalisation affecting the region and its wider implications on the peace and tranquility of the world. The SCO members emphasised the symbolic significance of 2026, which marks the 25th anniversary of the founding of the organisation and how its role is becoming more important than ever due to growing unpredictability and uncertainty.
summaryText:
# UPSC Study Note: **Operation Sindoor and SCO Counter-Terrorism Efforts**

## 1. CONTEXT

Operation Sindoor, held in April 2026, symbolised India’s determination in combating terrorism and extremism within its own borders and beyond. The event unfolded during the SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting in Kyrgyzstan, where Shri Rajnath Singh, the Home Secretary, emphasised the need for unity against terrorism, separatism, and extremism. This was particularly important given the evolving threat landscape where terrorism is no longer confined to a single geography.

The focus of the meeting was to strengthen cooperation among SCO nations to address the root causes of terrorism—namely, the presence of safe havens and ideological support. The emphasis was on collective action, rejecting political exceptions, and ensuring that those aiding terrorists are dealt with decisively. This is vital as India, alongside other SCO members, aims to maintain stability and uphold peace in the region.

## 2. KEY FEATURES

Operation Sindoor showcased several critical features:
- **Collective Stance:** India’s call for a unified front against terrorism underscores the necessity of solidarity among SCO nations.
- **Focus on Root Causes:** Emphasis was placed not only on eliminating terrorist hideouts but also on addressing underlying issues like Indo-Tibetan and India-China tensions that facilitate infiltration into India.
- **Rejection of Political Exceptions:** The statement clearly rejected any leniency for political actors or entities supporting terrorism, thus reinforcing the principle of zero tolerance.
- **Promotion of Dialogue:** The dialogue among SCO nations aimed at ensuring regional peace and fostering mutual trust, critical for counter-terrorism efforts.
- **Symbolic Significance:** The joint statement held symbolic importance as it highlighted India's commitment to peace and stability in the region, marking a milestone due to the 2026 anniversary.

The meeting also recognized the evolving nature of conflict in South and Central Asia, emphasizing the urgency of a proactive approach to prevent radicalisation and extremism.

## 3. SIGNIFICANCE

Operation Sindoor signifies a crucial step in the SCO’s broader agenda of regional security and stability. As the world grapples with increasing threats from terrorism and extremism, India’s proactive stance is significant for a multipolar order.

The emphasis on eliminating safe havens is pertinent because terrorist networks often find refuge in certain countries. By addressing this issue, India, alongside other SCO members, is actively contributing to the global fight against terrorism—thereby preventing it from spilling over borders into other volatile regions.

Additionally, the focus on regional cooperation and dialogue strengthens India’s position in the SCO. This unilateralism, especially in the face of internal and external pressures, demonstrates India’s commitment to being a responsible stakeholder in regional and global affairs.

## 4. UPSC RELEVANCE

Operation Sindoor is highly relevant for UPSC preparations as it reflects the current landscape of counter-terrorism operations. Students must analyse how the event addresses the themes of national security, regional cooperation, and the fight against extremism as discussed in the previous UPSC syllabus.

The principles underscored—such as the rejection of political exceptions and the collective responsibility for regional peace—align well with UPSC’s topics like maintaining law and order, security policy, and international relations. Understanding the dynamics of terrorism and the need for a united front is essential for any aspirant aiming for an IAS/CBC or IPS.

Furthermore, the meeting highlights the importance of dialogue, mutual trust, and the use of diplomacy in maintaining peace. These concepts are recurrent in the UPSC study material and form the backbone of effective governance.

In summary, Operation Sindoor is more than a military operation—it is a statement of India’s commitment to global stability and a model for the SCO in addressing transnational threats. Analysing this episode helps in understanding the practical application of theoretical principles in modern diplomacy and security strategy.

headline: TRAI Assesses Network Quality Across Warangal City and adjoining areas in Telangana State (under Andhra Pradesh Licensed Service Area)
fullText:
Ministry of Communications

TRAI Assesses Network Quality Across Warangal City and adjoining areas in Telangana State (under Andhra Pradesh Licensed Service Area)

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) released its Independent Drive Test (IDT) findings for the Andhra Pradesh Licensed Service Area (LSA), covering extensive city of Warangal and surrounding areas in the month of March 2026. The drive tests conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office, Hyderabad, were designed to capture real-world mobile network performance across diverse usage environments- Urban Zones, Institutional Hotspots, rural residential areas etc.

Between 23rd March 2026 to 25th March 2026, TRAI teams conducted detailed tests for 375.3 Kms Warangal City drive test and 8 Hotspot locations. Technologies evaluated included 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G, reflecting the service experience of users across multiple handset capabilities. The findings of the IDT have been intimated to all the TSPs concerned.

a) Voice Services: Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Setup Time, Call Silence Rate, Speech Quality (MOS), Coverage.

The overall mobile network performance in Warangal city is summarized below:

Call Setup Success Rate: Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have 100 %, 96.11%, 97.95% and 99.08% call setup success rate respectively in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G).

b) Data Services: Download/Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate, and Video Streaming Delay.

Average download speed of Airtel (5G/4G/2G) is 161.18 Mbps, BSNL (4G/3G/2G) is 8.32 Mbps, RJIL (5G/4G) is 260.01 Mbps and VIL (4G/2G) is 27.83 Mbps.

CSSR: Call Setup Success Rate (in %), CST: Call Setup Time (in seconds), DCR: Drop Call Rate (in %) & MOS: Mean Opinion Score.

Call Setup Success Rate: Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have 100.00, 96.11%, 97.95% and 99.08% call setup success rate respectively in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G).

Call Setup Time: Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have call setup time of 0.93, 1.69, 0.77 and 0.67 seconds respectively in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G).

Drop Call Rate: Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have drop call rate of 0.00%, 17.87%, 0.70% and 0.00% respectively in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G).

Call Silence/Mute Rate: Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have silence call rate of 0.58%, 2.36%, 0.29% and 2.33% respectively in packet switched network (4G/5G).

Mean Opinion Score (MOS): Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL have average MOS of 3.98, 2.97, 4.46 and 3.76 respectively.

The Poor signal strength in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G) during voice testing has been observed in 0.68%, 22.12%, 1.54% & 7.16% of the City IDT route in case of Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL respectively.

Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed of Airtel (5G/4G/2G) is 161.18 Mbps, BSNL (4G/3G/2G) is 8.32 Mbps, RJIL (5G/4G) is 260.01 Mbps and VIL (4G/2G) is 27.83 Mbps.

Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed of Airtel (5G/4G/2G) is 25.35 Mbps, BSNL (4G/3G/2G) is 3.07 Mbps, RJIL (5G/4G) is 19.48 Mbps and VIL (4G/2G) is 9.02 Mbps.

Latency (Overall): Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL 50th percentile latency is 38.36 ms, 57.81 ms, 21.94 ms & 43.70 ms respectively.

Data performance - Hotspots (in Mbps):

Airtel- 4G D/L: 40.48 4G U/L: 16.72

5G D/L: 196.34 5G U/L: 36.71

BSNL- 4G D/L: 8.07 4G U/L: 7.12

RJIL- 4G D/L: 67.16 4G U/L: 14.40

5G D/L: 338.42 5G U/L: 20.49

VIL- 4G D/L: 38.01 4G U/L: 12.40

Note- “D/L” Download speed, “U/L” Upload speed

The Poor signal strength in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G) during data testing has been observed in 10.99%, 29.61%, 13.22% & 11.46% of the City IDT route in case of Airtel, BSNL, RJIL and VIL respectively.

In Warangal City, the assessment included the areas nearby Kazipet, Hasanparthy, Hanamkonda, Subedari, Warangal, Mamnoor, Ashalapally, Theegarajupally, Gandhi Nagar, Yelgur, Mondrai, Pallaguda, Machchapur, Ghanpur, Uppugal, Raghunathapalle, Zaffergadh, Dammannapet, Madikonda, Chinnapendyala, Inavolu, Yellanda, hasaparthy and Arepally etc.

TRAI also evaluated real-world conditions at 1. Girmajipet (Warangal Chowrastha), 2. Hanamkonda Bus Stand, 3. Kakatiya Medical College, 4. Kakatiya University, 5. MGM Hospital Warangal, 6. NIT Warangal, 7. Reddy Colony (Road No: 2) and 8. Warangal Railway Station in Warangal City to reflect stationary user experience.

The tests were conducted using TRAI suggested equipment and standardised protocols in real-time environments. The detailed report is available at TRAI website www.trai.gov.in. For any clarification/information, Shri B. Praveen Kumar, Advisor (Regional Office, Hyderabad) TRAI may be contacted on email: adv.hyderabad@trai.gov.in or at Tel. No. +91-40-23000761.
